business_interpretation: Number of employees captures labor-market conditions that may influence customer response.
Euribor rate captures interest-rate conditions that may affect deposit product attractiveness.
Campaign timing may influence response due to seasonal or business-cycle effects.
Contact type may influence customer accessibility and campaign effectiveness.
Consumer confidence index captures customer sentiment and economic confidence.
Days since previous contact may indicate the effect of recency on customer response.
Previous campaign outcome helps identify whether prior marketing success affects current response likelihood.
Age can capture lifecycle differences that affect campaign response.
Day of contact may affect customer availability and campaign performance.
Number of campaign contacts may indicate whether additional outreach improves or reduces response likelihood.
